target_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: I've always been so interested in building out stuff that, you know, essentially tries to act as a, like, crutch. Like a, like, essentially the equivalent of like a brain crutch for someone with adhd

source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: Vrijen shared his personal interest in building AI tools as brain crutches for ADHD—filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching
Vrijen shared advice from his edtech experience: avoid the death by a thousand cuts of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes
A cornerstone customer providing reliable revenue creates options—whether to scale, attract others, or seek investment
Chris Hanson aligning with Vrijen's recommendation to pursue paid linchpin pilot with a design partner who will pay and limit feature requests
Vrijen offered to make introductions once Chris locks down a linchpin school deal; invited Chris to join the Future of Higher Education group
Both founders share similar backgrounds transitioning from non-technical fields into tech product development, with Vrijen validating Chris's accomplishment
Vrijen introduced context engineering as the foundational practice: not prompting the AI directly, but cultivating the context it uses to generate desired outputs
He outlined three strategic levels of context engineering: Conversation (tactical), Environment (persistent context), Pipeline (external data integration)
Logan demonstrated deep analysis of 18 years of LinkedIn data using Claude Code, having exported 48 CSV files containing 5,000+ messages, connections, search history
embrace the unhobbling concept from the Anthropic/Claude team—recognizing the AI is capable of more than you initially realize
Ben Guo introduced Zo Computer as a managed version of Claude Code, giving the AI its own computer (managed cloud server)

source_display: Plan Files as Future of Coding
evidence: documenting AI conversations in plan files that mix prose and code in stepwise fashion. He positioned this as the future of coding
Positioning: Careerspan as a human data company. Video demo should be human data oriented—show the data collection, then the results
Reference to Eightfold lawsuit—positioning against publicly scraped data risks. Hook: This moment in time is important (regulatory pressure on third-party data)
Logan's insight: Why don't we make a 30-second look at the information we capture via Careerspan? A deck is not as impactful as watching it in action
Decision: 45-60 second demo video instead of pitch deck for outreach. Demo leads with scan functionality, not candidate experience
Value prop crystallized: Oh shit, I want that information. Key differentiator: opted-in, first-party candidate data
Core message: We are a great solution because we collect first-party data. Positioning refined from tool to human data company
